title: Production seed override accepts any ALLOW_SEED value, including false
reasoning: The production guard message says ALLOW_SEED=true is required, but the condition only checks whether ALLOW_SEED is truthy. In Node, values such as ALLOW_SEED=false, ALLOW_SEED=0, or ALLOW_SEED=no are all truthy strings, so the script proceeds in production and immediately deletes all rows from multiple tables before reseeding. That makes a misconfigured environment variable enough to trigger destructive production data loss.
reproduction: Run with NODE_ENV=production ALLOW_SEED=false node src/seeds/seed.js; the guard is bypassed and the DELETE statements execute.
recommendation: Require an explicit exact value such as process.env.ALLOW_SEED === 'true' before allowing production seeding, and fail closed for any other value.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are provided for the seed script or its production guard behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a unit/integration test that sets NODE_ENV=production and ALLOW_SEED=false, stubs the database client, invokes seed(), and asserts that no DELETE/ALTER/INSERT queries are executed.
minimumFixScope: Update the production guard in src/seeds/seed.js and add coverage for accepted and rejected ALLOW_SEED values.
